corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

top_candidates.sentence: For future work we hope to use similar techniques for weakly supervised image segmentation.
We introduce YOLO9000, a state-of-the-art, real-time object detection system that can detect over 9000 object categories.
We introduce YOLO9000, a state-of-the-art, real-time object detection system that can detect over 9000 object categories.
First we propose various improvements to the YOLO detection method, both novel and drawn from prior work.
The improved model, YOLOv2, is state-of-the-art on standard detection tasks like PASCAL VOC and COCO.
Using a novel, multi-scale training method the same YOLOv2 model can run at varying sizes, offering an easy tradeoff between speed and accuracy.
At 40 FPS, YOLOv2 gets 78.6 mAP, outperforming state-of-the-art methods like Faster R-CNN with ResNet and SSD while still running significantly faster.
Finally we propose a method to jointly train on object detection and classification.
Using this method we train YOLO9000 simultaneously on the COCO detection dataset and the ImageNet classification dataset.
We propose a new method to harness the large amount of classification data we already have and use it to expand the scope of current detection systems.
We also propose a joint training algorithm that allows us to train object detectors on both detection and classification data.
We also propose a joint training algorithm that allows us to train object detectors on both detection and classification data.
First we improve upon the base YOLO detection system to produce YOLOv2, a state-of-the-art, real-time detector.
Then we use our dataset combination method and joint training algorithm to train a model on more than 9000 classes from ImageNet as well as detection data from COCO.
Then we use our dataset combination method and joint training algorithm to train a model on more than 9000 classes from ImageNet as well as detection data from COCO.
It can also run at different resolutions for an easy tradeoff between speed and accuracy.
YOLOv2 is state-of-the-art and faster than other detection systems across a variety of detection datasets.
Furthermore, it can be run at a variety of image sizes to provide a smooth tradeoff between speed and accuracy.
YOLO9000 is a real-time framework for detection more than 9000 object categories by jointly optimizing detection and classification.
YOLO9000 is a strong step towards closing the dataset size gap between detection and classification.
Dataset combination using hierarchical classification would be useful in the classification and segmentation domains.
We introduce YOLOv2 and YOLO9000, real-time detection systems.
